summary:
The comparison involves two independent groups with normally distributed continuous outcomes; thus, an independent samples t-test is most suitable. This test evaluates whether the observed difference in average heights between boys and girls is statistically significant or due to random variation.

Final Answer: C

We are asked to summarize only the **key clinical points**, focusing solely on what’s essential regarding the statistical method choice based on the scenario provided — no extra explanations beyond that.

Correct summary:

To compare the mean heights of two independent groups of similar size at the same developmental stage, the appropriate statistical test is the independent samples t-test. It assesses whether any observed difference in average height between boys and girls reflects true population-level divergence or simply sampling variability.
